title: Unlimited hard ulimit with soft limit not supported
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

[aaa@r8cn01 ~]$ docker run --rm -it --ulimit nofile="2000:-1"  centos  /bin/sh
Error: ulimit option "nofile=2000:-1" requires name=SOFT:HARD, failed to be parsed: ulimit soft limit must be less than or equal to hard limit: 2000 > -1

**Describe the results you received:**
Here is error message I got:
Error: ulimit option "nofile=2000:-1" requires name=SOFT:HARD, failed to be parsed: ulimit soft limit must be less than or equal to hard limit: 2000 > -1

**Describe the results you expected:**
The  command should be executed successfully.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
[aaa@r8cn01 ~]$ podman version
Version:            1.4.2-stable2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.8
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
[aaa@r8cn01 ~]$ podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.8
  podman version: 1.4.2-stable2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.2-5.module_el8.1.0+237+63e26edc.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.1-dev, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "8"
  MemFree: 5214932992
  MemTotal: 6246227968
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-60.rc8.module_el8.1.0+237+63e26edc.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: r8cn01
  kernel: 4.18.0-147.el8.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 129h 31m 26.65s (Approximately 5.38 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/xianwu/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /podmanfs
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /tmp/run-33857
  VolumePath: /podmanfs/storage/volumes

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**
[aaa@r8cn01 ~]$ rpm -q podman
podman-1.4.2-5.module_el8.1.0+237+63e26edc.x86_64
[aaa@r8cn01 ~]$ lsb_release -a
LSB Version:	:core-4.1-amd64:core-4.1-noarch
Distributor ID:	CentOS
Description:	CentOS Linux release 8.1.1911 (Core)
Release:	8.1.1911
Codename:	Core
```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
vmware environment.

comments.body: Does the `-1` specifier work in Docker?
Were using the same parser as Docker, so I doubt it.

@mheon @raukadah 
-1 works in docker.

in docker 19.03 on ubuntu 18.04:

Does it work or doesn't it?

just tried on rhel 8.2,
it does NOT work either:
[root@lsf1x95 ~]# docker ps
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
[root@lsf1x95 ~]# docker run --rm -it --ulimit nofile=“2000:-1” centos /bin/sh
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
Trying to pull docker.io/library/centos...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 8a29a15cefae done
Copying config 470671670c done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: ulimit option “nofile=“2000:-1"” requires name=SOFT:HARD, failed to be parsed: strconv.ParseInt: parsing “-1”“: invalid syntax
[root@lsf1x95 ~]# rpm -qa|grep podman
podman-docker-1.6.4-1.module+el8.2.0+5182+3136e5d4.noarch
podman-1.6.4-1.module+el8.2.0+5182+3136e5d4.x86_64
podman-manpages-1.6.4-1.module+el8.2.0+5182+3136e5d4.noarch
cockpit-podman-11-1.module+el8.2.0+4966+a27241e9.noarch

@rhatdan

a mistake, here, the encoding of the double quote(") is not correct, it works on rhel 8.2(podman 1.6.4). but it does NOT work on rhel 8.1(podman 1.4.2)

[root@lsf1x95 ~]# docker run --rm -it --ulimit fsize=“40992000:-1” centos /bin/sh
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
sh-4.4# ps -ef
UID        PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
root         1     0  5 06:15 pts/0    00:00:00 /bin/sh
root         8     1  0 06:15 pts/0    00:00:00 ps -ef
sh-4.4# cat /proc/1/limits
Limit                     Soft Limit           Hard Limit           Units
Max cpu time              unlimited            unlimited            seconds
Max file size             40992000             unlimited            bytes

Ok so you are saying we have fixed this in the latest releases?
Closing issues, reopen if I am mistaken.
yes, the cli works in podman 1.6.4.
